operating_state: Full-year 2021 revenue of $22.9 billion decreased 3% year-on-year primarily due to divestitures of OneStim pressure pumping and North America low-flow artificial lift businesses. Excluding these divestitures, global revenue increased 3% with accelerating growth in the second half. Operations span 120+ countries with approximately 6% of revenue recognized under long-term construction-type contracts using percentage-of-completion methodology.
cost_structure: Full-year 2021 pretax operating margin of 15% expanded 450 basis points compared to 2020, primarily driven by divestiture of margin-dilutive North American businesses and reduced depreciation following 2020 asset impairment charges. Fourth-quarter pretax segment operating margin reached 16%, the highest quarterly level since 2015, largely due to accelerating digital sales. Long-term construction contracts require continuous estimation adjustments with expected losses recorded in full when probable.
material_events: Schlumberger deposited funds to satisfy $1.0 billion of Senior Notes due 2022 during Q4 2021, recording a $10 million charge, and repurchased all $665 million of Senior Notes due 2021 during Q2 2021. The company sold 9.5 million Liberty shares in Q4 2021, receiving $109 million in proceeds and recognizing a $28 million gain. Schlumberger received a $477 million federal tax refund in Q2 2021 relating to US net operating loss carrybacks under the CARES Act.
macro_exposures: Operations across 120+ countries with 85% of revenue generated internationally expose Schlumberger to political instability, currency fluctuations, trade sanctions, and regulatory changes across multiple tax jurisdictions. The energy transition poses risks if demand shifts faster than anticipated or if the company fails to adapt its technology portfolio effectively. Climate change regulations and cybersecurity incidents could disrupt operations given heavy dependence on digital technologies.
forward_outlook: Management expects material step-up in industry capital spending with double-digit growth in both international and North American markets for 2022. Oil demand projected to exceed pre-pandemic levels before end of 2022 and strengthen further in 2023. Capital investments expected between $1.9-2.0 billion in 2022. The company believes favorable market conditions will result in exceptional multiyear growth cycle with Schlumberger positioned to capitalize through reset operating leverage.
uncertainty_profile: Accounts receivable in Mexico totaled approximately $0.5 billion at December 31, 2021, with payment delays from the primary customer though no material write-offs historically. Goodwill impairment testing completed with qualitative assessment concluding fair value significantly exceeds carrying amounts for all reporting units. Tax audits across multiple jurisdictions may result in additional assessments with ultimate resolution potentially differing from current estimates.
